item.title: Commemorating the Takeover of Fort Lawton
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

The history of Discovery Park is inseparable from the bold activism of the Native community. In March 1970\, the Takeover of Fort Lawton sparked a movement that changed the landscape of Seattle forever.   When Fort Lawton was declared surplus land\, Native activists joined together to occupy the land with hopes of garnering a space for the community. While many remember the iconic March 8th fence scaling\, we gather this year to honor the second occupation where leaders of the movement heroically scaled the cliffs at Fort Lawton. This strategic move ultimately helped UIATF secure a permanent space for the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center.   Today\, we steward 20 acres at Daybreak Star\, but our connection to the 500-acre vision remains. Join us for a commemorative march starting where the second occupation took place and ending at Daybreak Star for guest speakers and lunch! March 7th\, 2026 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM Lunch will be served after the march at 1:00 PM at Daybreak Star Shuttles provided after lunch to North & South parking lots (see map) #DiscoveryPark #daybreakstarindianculturalcenter #SeattleHistory #NativeActivism #FortLawton #IndigenousRights #AmericanIndianMovement

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Fix-it Fair
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Bring your broken items to the Fix-it Fair. The PNA Fixers will diagnose and repair your item for free (parts may be extra) while also helping reduce waste in Seattle. At the same time\, you can bond with the rest of your local community and learn something new by watching the repair. Typical items include heaters\, sewing machines\, waffle irons\, blenders\, mixers\, electric bikes\, extension cords\, lamps\, audio gear\, and more. There are hardware stores nearby if you need to fetch parts. There is also a professional knife sharpener on hand\, and we expect to have sewists to mend and hem clothing. Walk-ins are welcome but it’s helpful if you can RSVP to let us know what you’re bringing: bit.ly/GreenwoodFix-itFair. The PNA Fixers meet regularly at Greenwood Hardware on the first Wednesday of every month\, 3–6 pm. \nFree\, but donations are appreciated. \n

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Crochet Class: Book Cover Granny Squares
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

While this class is beginner-friendly\, knowing the basics like a slip knot\, single crochet\, and double crochet will be necessary for this project. If you would like to join and don’t have these skills yet check out my beginner crochet class at Swoon City or find some good tutorials on YouTube. I taught myself to crochet before the Internet existed and have all the faith you can learn too! \nWe will make a classic granny square in this class\, but there are many variations of granny squares. Granny squares can create bags\, blankets\, sweaters\, and more! We will focus on making granny squares that resemble book covers. You can use the cover colors or even replicate the parts of the cover design! \nThis class will include all the colors necessary to create your book-themed granny square\, a guide to making granny squares\, and a hook. Please provide a picture of the cover you are looking to create to info@swoon.city after booking

From My Ballard

item.title: Kristian Bugge & Ruthie Dornfeld – 2 Fiddle masters play Danish & American music and more!
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Kristian Bugge (Denmark) and Ruthie Dornfeld (Seattle) will entertain us with a wide range of music including Danish traditional fiddle tunes and music from the other Nordic countries to old-time American and self-composed original tunes\, spiced up with a few Venezuelan waltzes.\nKristian and Ruthie first met each other in the Danish folk music circles in the mid-1990’s\, where Ruthie had been touring regularly and growing a reputation as a well-renowned artist and fiddler since the early 1980’s. Later when Kristian was a music student\, Ruthie was guest-teaching at the Carl Nielsen Academy of Music in Odense\, Denmark and Kristian made sure to take the opportunity to study with her while she was there. About ten years later they met again in and around Seattle when Kristian had started touring and performing in the states. This summer marked a new chapter in their collaboration and friendship as they started touring and recording as a duo for the first time. Expect everything from wild polkas and jigs to lyrical waltzes\, fiery reels and happy hopsas\, all served with humor and stories from their many years on the road. The doors open at 7pm for the 7:30 cocnert in the phinney Community Hall (lower Brick Building). Advance tickets are $26/$24 members of SFS\, PNA\, or Skandia\, students\, or seniors 65+/ $10 Youth to age 17; at the door $2 more for adults ($28/$26/$10).

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Unexpected Productions: Seattle Theatresports Improv
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Fridays & Saturdays from 9:30pm - 11pm Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast, funny, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show, a high-energy, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle, part game night, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head, performing games, stories, characters, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds, decide the winners, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking, bold choices, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show, unless your parents were super cool. Buy Tickets

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: Service Sunday
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Magnolia United Church of Christ\, 3555 W McGraw St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

This is the Sunday where we join in work projects around our community as we BE the church! There are multiple service project locations around Magnolia with projects ranging from making blankets for kids entering foster care to habitat restoration! Afterwards\, we will all meet in MUCC’s Pilgrim Hall for cocoa and soup. Some locations have limited spots so register here today! Here is the list of this year’s Projects: Project Linus: Upstairs in Pilgrim Hall we will have a team that will make fleece blankets for kids going into Foster Care.  No sewing experience required!  This project is great for folks of all ages. Magnolia Park Habitat Restoration: The team will work under the direction of Seattle Green to remove aggressive weeds and maintain areas where weeds have been removed.  All equipment provided. Discovery Park Habitat Restoration: The team at Discovery Park will be doing outdoor habitat restoration.  All equipment provided. Seattle Animal Shelter: The team at SAS will be doing projects in service of sheltered animals (Children must be 12+ and have adult supervision). Bookmarks for Inmates for Hagar’s Ministry: The team will making bookmarks at MUCC in the Parlor for women at the Washington Corrections Center for Women. Ballard Food Bank Hygiene Repack Prep: The team (working at Magnolia Presbyterian) will help to repack bulk Hygiene\, Pet\, and Dry Foods into smaller packages so that the Food Bank can keep its shelves stocked with these much needed items. Daybreak Star Cultural Center: Working at the Daybreak Star Cultural Center at Discovery Park\, participants at this site will be involved in outdoor gardening\, restoration and help with landscaping projects around the Center and in their Elder Garden.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: 2026 Seattle Sister Cities Soup Sampling
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum\, 2655 NW Market Street\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Celebrate International Women’s Day at the 2nd annual Seattle Sister Cities’ Soup Sampling\, sponsored by the Seattle Sister Cities Association in partnership with the National Nordic Museum. The Soup Sampling is a community event where volunteer cooks from 11 Seattle Sister Cities share popular international soups representing four continents. Guests need to bring only a smile and an interest in tasting new recipes and learning about diverse cultures. Please register for the event before March 1st; walk-in admission will not be available. \nThis is a cultural event that includes five soup tastings per entry. Donations of $10 per person are encouraged and benefit the nonprofit Seattle Sister Cities Association to support cultural\, educational\, and humanitarian programs. (When reserving tickets\, your donation will result in 1 admission ticket. Use the quantity selector to reserve tickets for additional attendees). From Seattle’s 20 sister cities\, the following will be represented at this event: Reykjavik\, Iceland; Bergen\, Norway; Pecs\, Hungary; Be’er Sheva\, Israel; Christchurch\, New Zealand; Mombasa\, Kenya; Sihanoukville\, Cambodia; Perugia\, Italy; Surabaya\, Indonesia; Hai Phong\, Vietnam; and Kobe\, Japan. \nAttendees are also encouraged to visit New Nordic: Cuisine\, Aesthetics and Place as well as other exhibits at the National Nordic Museum and will get 50% off admission on the day of this event

From My Ballard

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: Market to Table Tour & Cooking Class
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Atrium Kitchen, 93 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

“We wish we did this at the beginning of our trip!” “You’ve exceeded all of our expectations! And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: Improv Happy Hour
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Join us for Improv Happy Hour—where comedy is unscripted, unpredictable, and unbelievably fun! From movie mashups to literary spoofs and bold, experimental comedy, our quick-thinking performers turn your suggestions into hilarious scenes on the spot. This family-friendly show at the iconic Gum Wall in Pike Place Market guarantees a night of laughter, surprises, and improv magic you won’t forget. Bring your wildest ideas, your loudest laughs, and get ready for a one-of-a-kind comedy experience—because at Improv Happy Hour, anything can happen (and usually does)! Buy Tickets

From Pike Place Market Events

Event details

item.title: Show Your Game: An Improvised Game Show
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Sundays in March! A Comedy Game Show About Ridiculously Specific Expertise. Everyone is secretly an expert at something. In Show Your Game , professional improvisers and fearless audience volunteers step into the worlds of real-life experts and attempt to survive challenges designed by those experts. Expect strange trivia, impossible tasks, wildly specific knowledge, and the kind of confidence that only comes from being completely wrong in public. Maybe it’s acupuncture. Maybe it’s panic packing. Maybe it’s Gilmore Girls . Maybe it’s finding creative new places to lose your keys. Inspired by the chaotic brilliance of Taskmaster , the clever curiosity of QI , and the unpredictable madness of Game Changer , every show is completely improvised and completely different. Past topics have included haunting, bubbles, bedtime routines, public art, computer programming, low-income housing, tabletop RPG streaming, Veggie Tales, housesitting disasters, and one unforgettable investigation into a s

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: The Production: An Improvised Mockumentary
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Sundays at 7pm Every week, a brand-new workplace. A fresh batch of quirky coworkers. And—worst of all—a different boss. Step inside the chaos of an unpredictable workday as the Unexpected Productions cast improvises an all-new, on-the-spot mockumentary. From the office know-it-all to the slacker skating by, you'll meet the characters you love to hate—and hate to admit you recognize. No script, no second takes—just pure, unscripted workplace mayhem. Buy Tickets

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: No Strings Attached: An Improvised Puppet Show for Grownups
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Sundays at 7pm At Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater , puppets let their hair down (or strings loose) in No Strings Attached —a hilarious, adults improv puppet show. After a long day entertaining the kids, these puppets are ready to play—singing bawdy songs, cracking jokes, and getting up to all kinds of outrageous fun. And the best part? You call the shots with your suggestions! Don’t miss this one-of-a-kind comedy show at Pike Place Market. It’s puppets, it’s improv, and it’s guaranteed to make you laugh. THIS SHOW CONTAINS MATURE CONTENT, LANGUAGE, AND SITUATIONS Buy Tickets

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

item.title: Homebuyer Workshops
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Join us to learn the steps to home ownership and the resources that help pave the way! During part one\, we’ll cover the home buying process and best practices using recently sold homes as examples. Plus\, an informative Q&A with a home inspector. During part two\, we’ll dive deeper into available downpayment assistance (DPA) programs. You’re welcome to attend part one or both sessions. \nWe meet at 6pm every 2nd Monday of the month in the Phinney Center\, Blue Building. Snacks and NA drinks provided. Please RSVP at caitlin.lakere.com.

item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

Monday, March 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Join Dr. Jim deMaine\, a pulmonary/critical care physician\, and Rebecca Crichton\, Executive Director of Northwest Center for Creative Aging\, to discuss our choices when seriously ill. Do advance directives really work? How can we advocate for ourselves and our loved ones? Jim draws from his book Facing Death: Finding Dignity\, Hope\, and Healing at the End\, a memoir about helping us plan for a more peaceful\, healing death.

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

Create your own ceramic wall pockets—perfect for fresh stems\, dried flowers\, or holding small everyday items. item.title: Industry Talks: Small Press Publishing with Homeward Books
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Join us at Hugo House’s Lapis Theater for Industry Talks: Careers in the Literary Arts\, an engaging evening that explores the many professional paths within the world of writing\, publishing\, and literary production. This event brings together authors\, editors\, publishers\, literary agents\, and other industry professionals for insightful conversations about their work and the evolving landscape of the literary arts. Attendees will gain exposure to the diverse opportunities available across the field—whether in creative writing\, editing\, marketing\, or event programming and learn how different roles contribute to the broader literary ecosystem. The evening will feature a moderated discussion\, followed by an open Q&A session and networking opportunities\, offering a welcoming space for writers\, readers\, and anyone passionate about storytelling to connect and exchange ideas. Who Should Attend: Members of the public interested in literature\, writing\, publishing\, and creative careers. Event Schedule: ·     6:30 PM: Hugo House Intro ·     6:35 PM: Industry Professional Intro ·     6:40-7:40 PM: Lecture ·     7:45 PM: Q&A from the audience ·     8:00 PM-8:30 PM: Signing/Merch booth/Meet and Greet/Networking Author J.M. Sidorova and founding editor Rachel Sobel discuss the logistics\, challenges\, and delights of running a small press\, publishing your work with big and small publishers\, and connecting with the greater books world. We’ll have a special sneak preview of the first Homeward Books title\, The Witch of Prague\, with copies available for purchase in an exclusive pre-release opportunity! Click here to preorder The Witch of Prague!

From Hugo House

item.title: Evelyn Iritani with Frank Abe
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Across the water from Seattle\, you can visit the Bainbridge Island Japanese American Exclusion Memorial. It’s a place to honor and learn from the past. Evelyn Iritani\, a longtime Seattle resident and Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist\, wants to remember – and learn from – another\, lesser-known story from World War II. In her book\, Safe Passage\, she reveals the dramatic\, behind-the-scenes efforts to bring U.S. and Japanese citizens home from enemy land. \nIn 1943\, during some of the Pacific theater’s bloodiest battles\, the United States and Japan coordinated the exchange of civilians caught on the wrong side of the battlefield after Pearl Harbor. Nearly fifteen hundred Allied civilians trapped in Asia\, mostly U.S. citizens\, sailed through dangerous waters to India\, where they were traded for Japanese immigrants sent from the U.S. The fate of the more than ten thousand U.S. civilians left behind in Asia rested on the success of this endeavor.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Jason Davis\, the Greenwood Branch Adult Librarian\, joins us to talk about all of the behind-the-scenes systems that keep Seattle’s libraries running smoothly. Come learn about all the steps that occur when you request a hold\, how interlibrary loans travel around\, how extensive the collection really is\, how books are selected for different branches\, what’s in the special collections\, and more! item.title: Write with Hugo House with Jeanine Walker
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 6 PM

In this beginner-friendly writing class\, we’ll focus on generative writing through poetry prompts. The session will begin with introductions and a short warm-up\, after which we’ll read two separate poems and write in response. At times\, you'll have the opportunity to share words and ideas with other writers in small groups. You'll leave with 2-3 new drafts of poems. Designed for all writers\, this group supports poets as well those working in other genres\, as the skills practiced in poetry can be thought of as “cross-training” for memoir and fiction\, too. \nAbout Write with Hugo House \nWrite with Hugo House is a free drop-in writing circle for adults of all ages and genres of writing. Bring something you’re working on\, or just come ready to write. You will have the opportunity to share your work and get feedback—but only if you want to (no pressure). You can also use this time to increase productivity on your current work in progress\, surrounded by fellow writers.

From Hugo House

item.title: PNA Woodshop\, dedicated to Bill Fenimore
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Boutique Bellydancersize Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Explore the ancient & mysterious art of belly dancing\, with Boutique Bellydancersize classes! Specialized classes. Superior core workout. Strengthens & coordinates muscle groups in the trunk\, spine & arms. Improves posture & flexibility. Students will learn the basic steps\, undulations\, shimmies\, isolations\, tuxseem and more. All Ages. Group classes at Phinney Center. A focused gym that provides a sense of connection. Zaphara’s classes go beyond physical fitness\, including a broader sense of self care\, including sleep\, nutrition\, skin health\, stress management & socializing. Starts the 1st Thursday of the Month 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm – Beginners 7:45 pm – 9:00 pm Intermediate – Advanced 4 weeks: $80\, Drop-ins: $27 per class Taught by Zaphara\, Master Bellydance Instructor & Performer Sign up and pay online: bellydancingbyzaphara.com/classes

item.title: From Rails to Trails Film Screening
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

If you have ever ridden on the Burke-Gilman Trail\, you’ve traversed part of a vast\, 3\,700-mile network known as the Great American Rail-Trail. The ambitious project of converting the nation’s abandoned rail lines into public corridors\, supported by more than a century of advocacy\, is the subject of a recent PBS documentary\, From Rails to Trails. \nNarrated by actor Edward Norton\, the film tells the story of one of the most unlikely social movements in American history. Join MOHAI and Cascade Bicycle Club for a special showing of the film. Tyler Vasquez\, Seattle Policy and Advocacy Officer\, will serve as the event’s MC\, bringing energy while highlighting the bike lanes delivered over the past five years and sharing a vision for the future of biking across the Puget Sound. He will also engage with the audience by answering questions about Seattle’s Bicycle Network and discussing how we can carry forward the momentum built by the advocates who came before him. \nFrom 6-7pm\, celebrate and explore Riding Together: 135 Years of Cycling in Seattle\, MOHAI’s current exhibition on display through April 26.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Crowdsource Choir
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Crowdsource Choirs continues their Sing Seattle Songs theme—and once again\, you help shape the show by choosing the music. The songs may be born in Seattle\, inspired by Seattle\, or made iconic through Seattle films and stories—but the final setlist will emerge from your votes. Choose the songs you want to sing on by voting here. Your selections will directly influence what we learn\, layer\, and sing together in the room. Town Hall is the perfect home for this gathering—a civic space where the people of Seattle come together to express who we are\, where we come from\, and how our stories keep evolving. This night becomes a shared act of remembrance and re-creation\, sung by the city itself. No rehearsals. No sheet music. No choir robes. Just a room full of voices\, Seattle pride\, and the soundtrack of the city we love—raised together in harmony. You help choose the songs. We become the choir.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Kids Embroidery Class: Fruit
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Try embroidery with this beginner workshop. Kids (10+) will learn how to embroider fruits using 3 beginner embroidery stitches. This class is great for kids looking to get started with hand embroidery. They’ll learn how to place their fabric in the hoop\, transfer the pattern\, split apart their thread\, and stitch the satin stitch\, back stitch\, and sead stitch. Each attendee will receive a full kit that includes: a wooden embroidery hoop\, fruit transfer designs\, an embroidery needle\, full skeins of embroidery thread\, cream cotton fabric\, and printed instructions.

From My Ballard

item.title: The Swapanistas Present: Swap! A Women's Clothing Exchange
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Phinney Neighborhood Center\, 6532 Phinney Ave N Room 7\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Are you ready to swap up your style? Then join us for Swap 2026! Swap is an amazing event where you let go of your gently used clothing and accessories and trade them in for items that make you feel brand new\, while having fun and supporting a good cause: proceeds and clothing donations benefit local organizations for underserved women and kids. We are excited to announce that this year’s event is being hosted in partnership with the Phinney Neighborhood Association\, truly making this a community event! ​​Come check it out\, and bring your friends! Swap: Saturday March 14th\, 10 AM – 1:30 PM Item Drop-off: Friday March 13th\, 5 PM – 8 PM For tickets and more info\, please visit our website: https://www.swapanistas.org/ Please email us with questions: swapanistas@gmail.com Hope to see you there!

From My Ballard

item.title: Art Class: Nature Journaling & basics of making botanical Inks
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Pause and see the natural world more closely through nature journaling and botanical inks. In this gentle\, hands-on class\, you’ll learn the basics of making ink from flowers and use these plant-based pigments to start a nature journal. You’ll also learn how considerations like paper and additives can give you different and exciting colors. All materials are included and each participant will take home a small sketchbook that we make in class and a 15ml pot of handmade floral ink—an invitation to notice new growth\, returning light\, and the unfolding of spring.

From My Ballard

item.title: American Echoes
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Solstice Symphony Orchestra returns on March 14 with a FREE Saturday evening concert. This performance also marks the debut of Solstice Camerata\, a new chamber-orchestra ensemble dedicated to repertoire written for smaller orchestral forces. \nAdmission is free; tickets are required. \nTitled American Echoes\, the concert will explore the “American sound” across time\, space\, and perspectives. The program opens with Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart’s Symphony No. 25\, which was composed the same year that the Boston Tea Party took place in the US (1773)\, situating its urgency and unrest within a wider moment of philosophical and political upheaval. The program then turns to four unique American voices from different eras and backgrounds — Charles Ives\, Florence Price\, Caroline Shaw\, and Aaron Copland — whose music engages with shared themes of doubt and devotion\, inheritance and reinvention\, intimacy and expansiveness. \nPerformed by a reduced ensemble in a chamber-orchestra setting\, American Echoes invites close listening and rewards reflection. Admission is free and open to all; tickets are required and will be available in advance. \nThis program is supported\, in part\, by a grant from the Washington State Arts Commission.

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: Burl Dirkman: Power Thru It!
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

From the mind of Brett Hamil\, the cartoonist behind Doom Loop and co-producer of Joketellers Union\, comes an utterly unique talk show. Starring Hamil’s wistful handyman alter ego Burl Dirkman\, Power Thru It! with Burl Dirkman will take the audience on a comedic journey of self-discovery\, featuring interviews with local luminaries J. Kenji López-Alt and Tomo Nakayama\, and cathartic activities designed to help you access your long-neglected emotional core. Prepare to cry real tears of joy!

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Musical Plot Twists
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Every piece has a new story to tell. \nSeattle Festival Orchestra’s winter concert invites you into a world where music evolves\, surprises\, and tells bold stories. In her third collaboration with SFO\, composer and cellist Olivia Marckx presents a newly reimagined version of Night Terrace\, a vivid work brimming with mystery and texture. Korngold’s Violin Concerto\, brought to life by SFO concertmaster Luke Fitzpatrick\, first renowned for its dramatic and cinematic flair\, now stands as a beloved staple of the violin repertoire. And Atterberg’s sweeping Second Symphony\, expanded and reshaped after its premiere\, proves that even completed works can keep growing. A program of compelling characters\, unexpected turns\, and music that sparks the imagination.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Whose Crit Is It\, Anyway?
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 4 PM

Inspired by improv shows and Dungeons & Dragons\, Whose Crit is It Anyway combines them into a performance with a cast of professional actors that will take on the characters you create. Choose their adventure and guide their actions\, and if you choose wisely\, they might even survive!  If you’ve never played Dungeons & Dragons\, never fear\, we’re using a simplified ruleset\, and we’ll have D&D veterans there to guide you every step of the way. If you’re a veteran player yourself\, come experience a completely new way to play. In addition to having fun\, learn valuable writing and storytelling skills through the medium of D&D: Collaborative Storytelling: Learn how to work together as part of a creative team and how to draw inspiration from each other Character Development: Get into the mind of your character and how they would act in various situations Improvisation and Creativity: Stories can change as the characters make decisions\, learn how to improvise and stay true to the story\, even in the face of the unexpected Your night will begin by being divided into five groups\, one for each of our actors\, plus one that will help the Dungeon Master craft the experience. You will select the abilities of the premade characters provided\, and then dive into the story. Your suggestions can guide their actions\, which you’ll watch them perform live on stage. With the input of the audience\, even our Dungeon Master has no idea where this story might end up! Forge the Legend! Get your tickets now! Must be 18 years of age or older for entry.

From Hugo House

item.title: Matthew Sutton with Bill Radke
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Whether or not you call yourself religious\, there’s no denying that religion has an impact on society across the continents. And there is no faith more dominant than Christianity in the United States today. Washington State University professor and historian Matthew A. Sutton can show you just exactly how evangelical Christianity entwines itself with all aspects of the country. Drawing from his book\, Chosen Land: How Christianity Made America and Americans Remade Christianity\, Sutton chronicles Christians’ five-hundred-year endeavor to turn the U.S. into their version of the kingdom of God.

item.title: David Pogue
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Although the present ubiquity of Apple is well-known\, how much do you know about its past? Just in time for Apple’s 50th anniversary\, author and CBS correspondent David Pogue tells the history of the iconic company: how it was born\, nearly died\, and was revived under the legendary Steve Jobs\, and how it later became the most iconic brand in the world. \nOn April 1\, 1976\, two men in their 20s\, both named Steve\, founded a modest startup. Their goal was to bring the power of computers to everyone. Over the next five decades\, Apple revolutionized the tech landscape\, introducing the public to many products that we use today and marrying technology with design. \nMarking this 50th anniversary milestone is Apple: The First 50 Years\, a 600-page\, definitive account of the tech giant. \nAt this very special event\, author David Pogue will share what he learned from interviewing 150 current and former Apple executives\, designers\, and engineers. He’ll reveal\, for the first time\, some amazing origin stories (Apple II\, iMac\, iPod\, iPhone). He’ll draw some counterintuitive but powerful lessons from Apple’s unlikely rise\, fall\, and rise. And he’ll punctuate the evening with Apple song parodies at the piano (yes\, really).

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Works in Progress
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Works in Progress is Hugo House's open mic series inclusive of diverse formats and storytelling crafts. Read your work—poetry\, fiction\, essays\, memoirs\, plays\, music\, comedy\, and more—and connect with our literary community!  Works in Progress is a low-pressure\, welcoming environment for readers and guests of all skill levels and literary experience. We especially encourage BIPOC\, LGBTQIA2S+\, and other marginalized voices to take the mic! Doors open at 6 pm PT for interested writers who want to come early\, carve out some sacred writing time\, and share ideas on craft and content. This open\, unstructured space is intended to create opportunities for folks to build community and enjoy some quality time writing their own works-in-progress!  Open mic begins at 7 pm PT. – Reading is open to all! – You don’t have to read. Guests and audience members are encouraged to attend! – Sign-up for reading begins on-site when doors open at 6 pm and continues until the open mic begins at 7 pm. – Only one sign-up per person. – The order for the 20 reading slots will be randomly assigned. You will be notified prior to the reading of your slot. – Each reader has 5 minutes to read. Be mindful of your excerpt length\, and practice beforehand if you can! Works in Progress is sustained through suggested donations from attendees. Please consider making a donation to Hugo House and the Works in Progress open mic program when you register. Thank you! ﻿Free admission to this event is made possible with support from 4Culture.

From Hugo House

item.title: Cindy Cohn
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

As we cascade further into the digital age\, concerns over privacy and data security continue to rise with increasing urgency. As artificial intelligence expands its reach\, vast aggregates of personal data are constantly being mined to refine future models. But\, the fight for digital privacy is as old as the digital age itself. In Privacy’s Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance\, author and Digital Rights Activist\, Cindy Cohn\, chronicles her career-long battle to preserve our right to privacy online. \nPart memoir and part legal history for a general audience\, Privacy’s Defender reminds the reader just how hard-won the privacy rights we enjoy today were. Cohn stresses the societal importance of digital privacy\, citing its role in combatting authoritarianism\, organizing public protests\, and reinforcing other human rights as well.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Business Over Lunch
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Aegis Living at Rodgers Park\, 2900 3rd Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Know Your Customer\, Grow Your Community: How to Attract\, Serve\, and Keep the Right Audience Order your tickets HERE! Topic Description: Understanding your audience is the foundation of effective marketing\, sales\, and customer experience—but many small business owners are still trying to speak to “everyone.” This session helps entrepreneurs clearly identify who their ideal customer is\, what they truly need\, and how to connect with them in an authentic way. Attendees will leave with clarity and confidence to align their messaging\, offers\, and outreach with the people they’re best equipped to serve. Why Attend (Value Points): Clarify who your ideal customer actually is—and who they are not Identify customer pain points that drive buying decisions Strengthen your messaging so it resonates instead of getting ignored Build deeper trust and loyalty with the right audience Improve marketing results without increasing your budget Reduce overwhelm by focusing your efforts more strategically Align your products or services with real customer needs Increase referrals by serving a clearly defined community Gain confidence in how you show up and communicate your value

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Teaching Writing Inside of a Jail: An Info Session With Golf Pencil Group
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Join us online with Hugo House for an informational session about Golf Pencil Group (GPG)\, an organization that offers creative writing classes for people incarcerated at the King County Jail in downtown Seattle. This free online event will feature teaching artists along with the current and former Directors of the program who will offer an overview of how GPG has grown as an organization\, the impact they have made\, and where they hope to go in the future. Attendees will gain insight into the group's work serving writers behind bars will hear stories about the transformational power of the pen (or rather\, the golf pencil in this case.) Golf Pencil Group will also share opportunities on how people can become involved in this work\, whether with GPG itself or with like-minded groups. A Q&A session will follow. The evening will feature a moderated discussion\, followed by an open Q&A session and networking opportunities\, offering a welcoming space for writers\, readers\, and anyone passionate about storytelling to connect and exchange ideas. Who Should Attend: Members of the public interested in teaching writing\, service to others\, carceral education\, and/or finding creativity in difficult places Location: Online — Zoom Cost — Free Hosted by: Golf Pencil Group\, with support from Hugo House Time: 6-7 PM Event Schedule: ·  6:00-6:15 PM: Golf Pencil Group Intro – Welcome\, Who we are\, What we do\, and How ·  6:15-6:30 PM: Personal Stories from teachers ·  6:30-6:40 PM: Ways to get involved ·  6:40-7:00 PM: Q&A from the audience ·   7:00 PM: Event Concludes Team Bios: Kayla Blau (she/her) is a writer and facilitator. Her writing can be found in the South Seattle Emerald\, The Stranger\, The Seventh Wave\, and Crosscut\, among others. She loves nothing more than a hole-in-the-wall restaurant and a good book.  Danielle Hayden is a multi-genre writer\, teaching artist\, and the current Director of Golf Pencil Group. Jared Leising is a poet and the author the chapbook The Widows and Orphans of Winesburg\, Ohio. He holds an MFA from the University of Houston and teaches English at Cascadia College and creative writing at the King County Jail with Golf Pencil Group. Tasha Watson (they/she) is a queer\, nerdy\, neurodivergent writer\, professor\, and activist in the Seattle area. They love collaborative storytelling\, poetry\, live music\, and the macabre. Jay Aquinas Thompson (they/he) is a poet\, essayist\, and memoirist\, as well as a teacher of creative writing to public school students and women surviving incarceration. They are also the author of the 2024 memoir is titled The Resurrection Appearances: Fragments of a Daybook.

From Hugo House

Event details

item.title: Known Wishes with Jared Strote and ﻿Wendy Norman
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: The Mehdi Reading Room\, 1119 8th Ave\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Is the series The Pitt reality or fiction? \nIn the series The Pitt there are several episodes where doctors discuss a patient’s wishes with loved ones when the patient cannot communicate. You can often hear forms referenced like Advance Directives\, Do Not Resuscitate Orders and the Portable Order for Life Sustaining Treatment (POLST). These forms are all methods to limit or guide the treatment a patient does or does not want in an ER\, depending on how serious their injury or illness is and the risk to their quality of life. To understand the role these forms can play to protect the wishes or you or your loved ones\, join Wendy Norman from Known Wishes as she guides you through how to prepare in advance of a healthcare crisis.

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: History Café: Seattle Mystic Al Hubbard
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join Brad Holden\, author of Seattle Mystic Alfred M. Hubbard for a discussion of one of Seattle’s most mysterious figures. Hubbard first appeared in the papers in 1919 for his supposed invention of a perpetual motion machine that harnessed energy from Earth’s atmosphere. From there\, he transformed himself into a bootlegger\, radio pioneer\, top-secret spy\, and eventually “the Johnny Appleseed of LSD”. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–light food and drink will be available to purchase in the atrium. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: ReWilding Seattle Gardens
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join local beekeepers\, gardeners\, and native plant experts in a conversation about turning your own backyard into a native ecosystem oasis. Learn about the benefits of mason bees\, the importance of best-gardening practices to protect Puget Sound salmon\, and how you can make a difference in keeping our city climate change resilient. \nFeaturing Dave Hunter\, author of Mason Bee Revolution: How the Hardest Working Bee Can Save the World One Backyard at a Time\, along with panelists Jessi Bloom and Bill Thorness\, this event hopes to empower everyone to play a part in rebuilding healthy pollinator networks — and securing a thriving\, sustainable planet.

item.title: Kids On The Brink: Raising Emotionally Healthy Youth
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Zoom

Kids On The Brink: Raising Emotionally Healthy Youth In this event\, Donna Jackson Nakazawa will combine cutting-edge science\, real student voices\, and practical tools to help adults better support today’s youth. Donna will cover: – The latest research on female brain development – The unprecedented challenges facing teens today and why so many are in crisis – How these factors impact youth mental health – Why social stress\, in particular\, is the most damaging type of stress for kids – How regulating ourselves helps our kids regulate their emotions Participants will gain: – Actionable strategies parents\, families\, and organizations can utilize to better support our youth – 12 science-based steps for talking with youth in neuroprotective ways and building healthier parent-child connection Donna will also share highlights from her national Post-It Note Project\, featuring insights from thousands of middle and high school students about what they wish their parents and other adults understood—and did—differently. This event will be moderated by Christy Keating from The Heartful Parent Collective.

From My Ballard

item.title: Silent Reading Night
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6 PM

Silent Reading Night – March Edition Date: Thursday\, March 19th Time: 6:00 PM – 9:00 PM Theme: Pages Shared: A Community Book Exchange We wanted to celebrate this month’s silent reading night with a community book exchange themed reading night! Feathered Pages will be tabling for this event. Event Activities: Community Book Swap To participate in the book swap\, let the front desk know upon arrival and receive your raffle ticket. One book = 1 ticket. Feel free to bring as many books as you would like to swap! Grab some wrapping paper from the front desk with a blank note card that answers one of these two prompts: “Why I loved this book…” or “describe this book in a sentence..” Wrap up the book and tape the note card to the cover with the answered prompt. Drop books at categorized area on the stage Fiction Nonfiction Romance Fantasy/Sci-Fi Memoir Raffle Announcement (at 8:30 PM) There will be up to 5 winners! The prize is a Hugo House tote with books inside! Event Itinerary 6:00 PM – Mix & Mingle Grab a cup of tea and settle in. Meet fellow readers and share what book you’re currently enjoying. BYOM – Bring Your Own Mug! Hot water will be available. 6:30 PM – Silent Reading Begins Settle into your cozy reading spot and dive into your book. Enjoy the quiet atmosphere of reading in community. 7:30 PM – Reading Break Chat with your neighbor! Ask them what they are reading. Grab a snack or drink at the bar Check out Feathered Pages' book table 7:40 PM – Break Ends Head back to the Lapis Theater and do more reading. 8:30 PM – Social Time & Book Chats Share thoughts on your reads\, favorite passages\, or just connect with fellow book lovers. 3 raffle winners are announced. 9:00 PM – Event Concludes This is our monthly Silent Reading Night\, each month we will have a new theme to inspire your reading. Rediscover the joy of reading in good company at the Hugo House. We can’t wait to read with you!

From Hugo House

Event details

item.title: Book Release Party: A Change of Pace
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Join Swoon City & Generous Press to celebrate the launch of “A Change of Pace” by J.A. Stevens. 📚 Copy of A Change of Pace with signed bookplate Included in the ticket price Event will feature: 📖 A reading by Generous Press Co-founders 🍓Light bites and refreshments 🎭 Craft your own mask 📸 Photobooth 👯 Have a meet-cute with other bookish folks 👗 Regency dress is encouraged Description of A Change of Pace: This sapphic historical romance brings “pride without prejudice” to Regency London. Although cavalier with love\, the famously rakeish Miss Georgina Pace is deeply concerned for the well being of others. And so when she learns that dear innocent Mr. Coombes has been targeted by a corrupt gaming house\, she will stop at nothing to protect him. Miss Pace’s quest for justice leads her to the enigmatic Lady Mortimer\, who offers assistance while keeping her cards close to her chest. Will Miss Pace risk it all—including her heart? A Change of Pace reimagines a Regency London free of the entrenched systems of oppression we expect\, putting a speculative and hopeful lens onto the past.

From My Ballard

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Speaking of Seattle: Immigrant Rights Are Human Rights
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

At a moment when national politics are testing the boundaries of constitutional protections and human dignity\, local communities are asking a vital question: What can we do to protect one another? \nJoin Town Hall Seattle and The Stranger for the March 19 edition of the Speaking of Seattle civic conversation series\, an evening focused on immigrant rights\, community responsibility\, and the everyday actions that help safeguard our neighbors. \nThis timely conversation will explore how federal immigration enforcement policies ripple through local communities — and how ordinary people can respond with care\, courage\, and solidarity. Together\, we’ll examine what it means to treat immigrant rights as human rights\, and how community members can act lawfully\, safely\, and effectively when confronted with fear-based tactics and unconstitutional overreach.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: EMBROIDERY CLASS: Botanical Thread Painted Earrings
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Have you ever wanted to color with thread? In this class we’ll do just that! With hands-on guidance from fiber artist and author\, Melissa Galbraith\, you’ll enjoy a 3-hour workshop learning the basics of thread painting\, stitching on tulle fabric\, and stumpwork embroidery to create a pair of botanical earrings. No matter if you’re an experienced embroiderer or new to stitching\, in this class we’ll practice stitches needed to thread paint and create 3D ginkgo earrings. All materials are included for class to create wearable 3D embroidery.

From My Ballard

item.title: Washington Ensemble Theater Playwright Showcase
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 6 PM

WETWorks A Playwright-in-Residence program Conceived and Curated by Nelle Tankus and Anamaria Guerzon In Partnership with Hugo House FALL 2025- SPRING 2026 | Hugo House   WETWorks is our brand new Playwright-in-Residence program\, in partnership with Hugo House. Over the next year\, WET will collaborate with three playwrights for a three-month paid residency each. They will be provided space to write and a creative team\, culminating in a final presentation at Hugo House at the end of their tenure. WETWorks provides the tangible resources\, community connections\, and creative space needed for Seattle playwrights. With this program we hope to answer a call for Seattle based playwriting opportunity\, as well as echo the Pacific Northwest lens and experience back to ourselves. Playwright for March 20th play: Isabelle Bushue Play Name: Extraordinary Outstanding Luck Synopsis: A Chinese adoptee comes to terms with her childhood and explores how to live without knowing her origins. Buy tickets here! Event Schedule: Doors Open: 6:00 PM Event Start: 7:00 PM Event End: 9:00 PM

From Hugo House

item.title: Global Rhythms: Saha Gnawa
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Saha Gnawa brings North African futurism to the U.S.\, uniting the founders of Innov Gnawa with leading figures from New York City’s jazz and contemporary music scenes to reimagine modern Gnawa music. \nOften referred to as “Sufi Blues\,” Gnawa is rooted in Morocco’s ancient history. Over centuries\, infectious rhythms and polytheistic spiritual beliefs originating from West African slaves integrated with Islam and the indigenous culture of Morocco\, giving birth to a hybrid spiritual sect and musical community. The lyrics of Gnawa songs are prayers invoking saints and spirits for freedom and liberation. Gnawa music is revered and treasured throughout Morocco for its soulfulness and healing power. \nRefined via late-night sessions at LunÀtico in Bed-Stuy\, Brooklyn\, Saha Gnawa explores trance-like states through its intuitive interplay\, allowing ancestral rhythms and melodies to drift into unknown territories. What emerges is both an authentic performance of centuries-old Gnawa tradition and a bold step into new dimensions of sound — where traditional song forms dissolve into ecstatic dance tracks laced with subtle electronics. \nWatch: Saha Gnawa – Bacha Hamou / Baba Mimoun (Live at Brooklyn Bowl)

From Town Hall Seattle

item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Want to ride a motorcycle but don’t know where to start? Join Cascade Motorcycle Safety and Ride With Us for a chance to experience riding a motorcycle for free\, with professional Cascade Motorcycle Safety RiderCoaches helping you through each step. These 1-on-1\, 20-minute sessions are designed for total beginners\, with bikes and helmets provided. \nThis program is presented with the support of Cascade Motorcycle Safety. \nRide out the remainder of the day in MOHAI to see Kickstands Up! 125 Years of Motorcycling in the PNW. Program participants will receive a coupon for $5 off museum admission. \nAs of 2/18/26\, the location and time of this event has changed.

item.title: Spring Dance
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: ARC Dance Space\, 9250 14th Avenue NW\, Seattle\, 98117\, United States

ARC Dance presents its annual Spring Dance performances featuring early dances from the 17th and 18th century re-created by Dance Historian\, Anna Mansbridge. The program also includes excerpts from the classic “La fille mal gardée”\, a charming comedic ballet choreographed by Artistic Director Marie Chong. The complete ballet will premiere on May 30\, 2026 at Shorewood Performing Arts Center. \nThis family-friendly performance takes the audience on a beautiful progression through dance history\, from early court dances to one of the oldest story ballets still being performed today. \nMarch 21 and 22 at 4pm\nARC Performance Space in Ballard/Crown Hill\, 9250 14th Ave NW\nTickets available at arcdance.org

From My Ballard

Welcome to a Impossible Loves\, a romance made up on the spot from your suggestions. Laugh\, care\, and swoon with your friend or foe as Impossible Loves improvises a brand new romance just for you! \nDiscover Impossible Loves\, a team of improvisers creating new worlds and new romances from your suggestions. Each one-hour improvised play opens by asking the audience about times they fell in love. From those moments\, Impossible Loves will create a brand-new romance show. item.title: Ballard Bookworms Community Storytime
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Greenwood Public Library\, 8016 Greenwood Ave N\,\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Calling all bookworms! Ballard Preschool Co-op (BPC) is thrilled to present its FREE monthly Ballard Bookworms story time. Join BPC’s beloved Teacher Christine for a fun and engaging story time designed to spark a love of reading and build connections within our community. \nThis month our Special Guest is Emily Weissman (known online as Urb.Em.Sketches) a local urban sketcher\, author and illustrator. Emily’s watercolor paintings celebrate beloved neighborhood places that make Seattle extraordinary. She will be reading her children’s book\, My Home is With You\, a whimsical exploration of how home is not just a place\, but found in the hearts of those we love. \nBallard Bookworms is free and open to the public\, so be sure to invite your friends & neighbors! Recommended for ages 5 and under (older siblings welcome) \nThis program is organized by the Ballard Preschool Co-op and is held in a library branch meeting room. The Seattle Public Library does not sponsor or endorse this event.

From My Ballard

item.title: Ibram X. Kendi
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Ibram X. Kendi\, the National Book Award-winning historian of Stamped from the Beginning charts how “great replacement theory” has moved from the margins to become the most dominant political theory of our time — and what we can do to safeguard democracy from this insidious threat. \nWhat is great replacement theory? Variations on the theory have existed for centuries\, but it was given this name by a French novelist in 2011 who believed Black and Brown immigrants were “invading” Europe\, brought by shadowy elites to “replace” Europe’s White population. From there\, politicians and theorists — whether in the United States or the United Kingdom\, Germany or Chile\, Hungary or Australia — repackaged the conspiracy as a story of “globalists” welcoming “migrant criminals” and diversity initiatives to take away the jobs\, cultures\, electoral power\, and the very lives of White people. Over time\, great replacement theory has expanded the threat to include citizens\, men\, Jews\, Christians\, heterosexuals\, and ethnic majorities in countries as distinct as Russia\, El Salvador\, Brazil\, Italy\, and India. All are targeted with the message that they are under an existential attack that only a strongman can prevent. \nRecall the words chanted in Charlottesville\, Virginia\, but heard around the world: “You will not replace us!” Recall the string of mass shooters around the world—in Oslo and Christchurch\, Buffalo\, El Paso\, and Pittsburgh—who claimed their crimes were a defense against “White genocide.” Recall business and media figures cultivating anxiety and furor over demographic change. These incidents only scratch the surface of this ascendant idea: Popular and ruling politicians in every region of the world have been expressing some version of great replacement theory\, eroding democratic norms in the name of preventing demographic change and restoring national greatness. \nIn our fast-shifting political landscape\, most people are unfamiliar with this theory’s origins and its spread\, which isn’t a coincidence. In Chain of Ideas\, international bestselling author Ibram X. Kendi uses exacting and clear prose to uncover the roots of great replacement theory and its various mutations around the world. It is an unsettling but indispensable global history of how great replacement theory brought humanity into this authoritarian age — and how we can free ourselves from it.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: MOHAI Mercantile Literary Series - Seattle's Locks and Ship Canal
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

MOHAI Mercantile is proud to host the official book launch event for Seattle’s Locks and Ship Canal with local historians David B. Williams and Jennifer Ott! \nThis portable guide from UW Press and HistoryLink is an introduction to the engineering marvel of the Ballard locks\, and a primer on the history of the ship canal. Hear from the authors about the tumultuous history of one of Seattle’s defining projects\, and the dramatic changes it brought to the city’s economy\, neighborhoods\, and natural environment. \nDoors open at 6pm for music and mingling. Food and drink available for purchase. \nConversation and Q&A begin at 7pm\, followed by a book signing with the authors. \nPreorder your copy through the MOHAI Mercantile beginning in January 2026!

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: March Magnolia After Hours at Seattle Makers
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Makers\, 3012 16th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Our March After Hours is shaping up to be one of the most unique experiences we’ve offered. Join us on March 24th from 6:00–8:30pm as Seattle Makers opens their 12\,000+ square feet of creative space exclusively for our Chamber community. \nYou’ll get a guided tour through their incredible maker environment\, explore the endless possibilities for hands‑on creation\, and watch live t‑shirt printing in action. Even better — you’ll walk away with a custom t‑shirt you made yourself\, a fun reminder of the night. \nAlong the way\, you’ll meet fellow Chamber members\, learn the story behind one of our most innovative member businesses\, enjoy a little pizza\, and experience the kind of creative energy that makes Seattle Makers such a standout in our community. \nThis special group experience is offered to members for just $40 per person — a significant Chamber‑only discount. \n👉 Reserve your spot and join us for creativity\, connection\, and community.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Cate Osborn
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Comedian\, historian\, and bestselling author Cate Osborn brings a special program derived from her live touring show\, Wildly Unprepared. This evening features a curated portion of the performance\, exploring the strange and surprising history of magic\, spectacle\, and belief\, along with storytelling and conversation about curiosity\, culture\, and the stories we inherit. \nWhile this program draws from the larger theatrical show\, it is designed as a standalone event created especially for Town Hall audiences. The show will be followed by a Q&A with Cate.

From Town Hall Seattle

Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 11 AM

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. The plants grown here nourishes our elders\, youth\, families\, and ceremonies. Help us build a future rooted in culture\, care\, and connection! 🍓 \nQuestions? item.title: Comprehensive Plan Office Hours- Magnolia Public Library
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Magnolia Public Library\, 2801 34th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

From the Office of Councilman Kettle: \nRight now\, the Office of Planning and Community Development is putting together four specific subarea plans: the Downtown Regional Plan\, the Northgate plan\, and two Manufacturing-Industrial Center plans. All of these plans are intended to come to Council to be voted on in late summer-early fall\, and you can sign up here for OPCD updates. \nIn the meantime\, my office will be hosting several Comprehensive Plan-specific office hours in March and April. \nThese office hours will follow our standard rules: sign in\, each speaker / group gets 10-15 minutes (depending on how many people have signed up)\, and will be first come\, first serve. \nYou can use my Instagram or Facebook to track updates.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Gem Painting: Dazzling Book Covers Class at Swoon City
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

In this delightful 2 hour workshop\, you’ll learn how place gem rhinestones on the cover of your favorite book to create a dazzling design sure to be the star of your shelfie. \nOne note – you will likely NOT COMPLETE a book cover in two hours but you will be able to do the lettering or other design elements. However you will take home up to gems and other supplies to finish your project at home\, if you choose. \nYou can choose a book in the store which is valued $20 or less\, or you can bring your own book\, and receive at $15 store credit. \nAfter the workshop\, you’ll have all the skills you’ll need to continue your gem painting journey. No experience necessary\, beginners welcome. \nThis class will take place at Swoon City.

From My Ballard

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: It Goes On
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 7 PM

$3 wine & poetry Readings by Samar Abulhassan\, Kevin Emerson\, Ramon Isao\, and Arlene Naganawa Thursday\, March 26\, 7pm Doors at 6pm Hugo House is delighted to partner with poet Jeanine Walker and mixed media artist Aaron Counts to bring It Goes On to the Hugo House stage! It Goes On features $3 glasses of wine\, $12 bottles\, and invaluable words. March 26 stars poets Samar Abulhassan and Arelene Naganawa\, and fiction writers Kevin Emerson and Ramon Isao. Come on out to our cozy cabaret-style theater to partake in the words\, wine\, and full-on spring fun.

From Hugo House

item.title: Print Class: Block Printing on Clothing
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Have a stain on your favorite shirt that you need to cover up? Wanting to add a little extra flair to your favorite pair of jeans? Come learn block printing basics and print onto your clothes with Alessia! In this class you will learn the basics of block printing: -creating a design -carving your block -inking and getting a clean print Please bring your own garment(s) to print on and come prepared with an idea of what you’d like to print! The blocks we’ll be using are 4”x 6” – feel free to bring in a drawing or print-out and I’ll help you transfer it into your block. Or come with just an idea and I’ll help you translate it into something printable! Use our communal supplies in class\, or snag a block printing kit for 20% off if you buy it with your class ticket. Note: Textured fabrics like corduroy or knitted items are difficult to print on\, please bring something that’s nice and smooth (i.e. cotton t-shirt\, denim\, etc.)

From My Ballard

item.title: Intermediate Crochet Class: Mushroom Chapstick Holder
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

THIS IS AN INTERMEDIATE CLASS. BASIC CROCHET KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED. \nMake a crochet mushroom chapstick holder with Alessia! Attach it to your keychain and carry your chapstick with you wherever you go. Or use it as an ornament or bag charm! \nThis is an intermediate crochet class\, intended for folks who have done some crocheting before. These tiny mushrooms require us to work on a small scale\, which can be tricky for beginner crocheters. \nIf you want to learn how to crochet\, we recommend taking one of Anna’s classes first. \nWe will be using chains and single crochet to make these mini mushrooms\, so make sure you know those stitches. We’ll be using magic circles too\, but no worries if you haven’t done that before – we can teach you! All materials will be provided.

From My Ballard

Whoome is excited to bring Sing Along to Seattle for the first time. An unforgettable musical experience! This one-of-a-kind event invites you to take center stage\, singing timeless Arabic classics chosen by you\, our audience. With digital lyrics provided\, everyone gets to be the lead singer in this interactive musical celebration. Join Maple Arts Group in Seattle for an evening filled with joy\, connection\, and the magic of music. Don’t miss your chance to be part of this extraordinary sing-along experience! The evening will be presented by Maple Arts Group musicians from the Canadian Arabic Orchestra. Events are offered for informational\, entertainment\, and educational purposes only. Read Town Hall’s Program Content Policy.

item.title: Spelling Bee 2026
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Want to watch 90 middle-schoolers knock out words like éclaircissement\, palooka\, and quonk? So do we!  \nThe regional spelling bee brings together students from schools across King and Snohomish Counties who have already proven themselves to be fantastic spellers. This year’s bee will be moderated by Seattle Radio Theatre founder and KIRO Radio’s producer\, host\, and historian Feliks Banel. The winner will advance to the annual Scripps National Spelling Bee in Washington\, D.C. Join us to support our outstanding students as they grapple with high-level language and cheer them on as they try to take the championship in 2026!

From Town Hall Seattle
