item.title: Suzanne Simard with Lynda Mapes
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Forest ecologist Suzanne Simard has long been fascinated by the sharing of natural knowledge. From the interconnected root systems she studied in her book Finding the Mother Tree to her ongoing work as an educator\, Simard has learned to see the importance of cooperative efforts to share resources and knowledge. Joined in conversation by Seattle-based nature journalist Lynda Mapes\, Simard expands these connections into a considerate exploration of the elaborate cycles of forest ecosystems\, the challenges they currently face\, and the intergenerational value they can provide through her new book When the Forest Breathes: Renewal and Resilience in the Natural World. \nRaised in a family of loggers committed to sensible forest stewardship\, Simard has watched as modern practices and timber companies have left forests vulnerable to damage and depletion. In her research\, Simard explores the finely honed cycles of regeneration forests inherently use to maintain themselves. From mushrooms breaking down logs to dying elder trees passing their genetic knowledge to younger growth\, When the Forest Breathes presents these cycles as a key component in the protection and preservation of our forests. Working closely with Indigenous communities and the models of responsible forestry they’ve upheld over time\, Simard examines the damage caused by industrialization and wide-scale human intervention– particularly the impact on the overstory’s mother trees that are responsible for sharing intergenerational wisdom and supporting new growth.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Stephen Graham Jones
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Stephen Graham Jones is the author of bone-chilling novels that include The Only Good Indians\, My Heart Is a Chainsaw\, I Was a Teenage Slasher\, and most recently\, The Buffalo Hunter Hunter\, a historical horror novel tracing the life of a vampire who haunts the fields of the Blackfeet reservation looking for justice. \nA diary\, written in 1912 by a Lutheran pastor\, is discovered within a wall. What it unveils is a slow massacre\, a chain of events that goes back to 217 Blackfeet dead in the snow. Told in transcribed interviews by a Blackfeet named Good Stab\, who shares the narrative of his peculiar life over a series of confessional visits. This is an American Indian revenge story written by one of the new masters of horror\, Stephen Graham Jones.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: On Point Live with Meghna Chakrabarti
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join KUOW for a special live taping of On Point with Meghna Chakrabarti Tuesday\, March 31 at Town Hall Seattle. \nSeattle’s mayor\, socialist Katie Wilson\, won one of the tightest elections in the city’s history last year. Now\, three months into the job\, how is her progressive agenda taking shape — and what might national Democrats learn from her early leadership? \nJoin On Point host Meghna Chakrabarti on Tuesday\, March 31 at 7:30 p.m. at Town Hall Seattle as she sits down with Mayor Katie Wilson for a special live taping of On Point.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Interbay Summer Camps- The Little Gym
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Little Gym- Interbay\, 2213 15th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Interbay summer camps are 3 hours of Serious Fun for kiddos 3-10 years of age! Camp consists of 2 hours of gym time\, and 1 hour of snacks and craft time. Every week brings a new and exciting theme for your camper and we always have blocks\, books and coloring pages on standby for those campers that need a break. Camps are held Monday-Wednesday from 1-4pm. We offer special discounted pricing for members and camp pack purchases. Non member pricing is $75 for a single camp pass. More information on pack pricing can be found via our scannable QR code. Priority registration opens February 1st.

item.title: Kiliii Yüyan
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

When we think of environmental conservation\, we tend to think in terms of emerging technologies and increased data tracking. While technology is one piece of the puzzle\, we are also called to look back and take stock of information that has existed since time immemorial. In Indigenous communities\, knowledge of the environment and how to protect it has been acquired over countless generations. In Guardians of Life: Indigenous Science\, Indigenous Wisdom and Restoring the Planet\, Kiliii Yüyan presents a photographic journey that reveals Indigenous communities to be Earth’s most powerful protectors. \nYüyan’s photography is informed by the collective wisdom of more than two dozen Indigenous voices\, including activist Quannah Chasinghorse and Palauan statesman Tommy Remengesau Jr. Yüyan provides striking imagery to assist in telling the stories of how traditional knowledge and modern innovation come together as a planetary safeguard. Guardians of Life takes us all over the world\, from the coral reefs in Palau\, to Mongolia’s steppes\, to the Amazon rainforest\, even to beds of sea ice in Greenland.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Free First Thursday at Seattle Area Museums
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Various Seattle museums

Free admission at participating Seattle area museums on the first Thursday of every month. Includes SAM (10am–8pm), Seattle Asian Art Museum (10am–5pm), Burke Museum (10am–8pm), Museum of Flight (5–9pm), MOHAI (5–8pm), National Nordic Museum (10am–8pm), and Holocaust Center for Humanity (4–7pm).

From Free First Thursday at Seattle Area Museums

Event details

item.title: Living Voices: Winning Her Wings
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Joshua Green Foundation Theater\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

They flew planes. They fought prejudice. They made history. Winning Her Wings brings to life the groundbreaking Women Airforce Service Pilots (WASPs). Follow journeys of women who faced and broke barriers together\, fighting for justice in a time of global conflict and domestic prejudice. Discover how they soared—despite the odds. \nThis new multimedia performance by Living Voices blends live theatre and archival film to honor the women who served with courage. Premiering in 2026 for Women’s History Month. This event takes place during our Free First Thursday hours. Seats are first come first served. \nLiving Voices combines live theatrical performances with archival film\, turning history into a moving personal journey. This performance will take place in the Joshua Green Foundation Theater on MOHAI’s second floor.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Psychedelic Salon: Psychedelics & Disordered Eating
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

A conversation about the emerging potential of psychedelics\, particularly psilocybin\, in addressing eating disorders—a psychiatric area marked by persistent treatment challenges. This Salon dives into groundbreaking research showing how psychedelics disrupt entrenched thought patterns\, ease cognitive rigidity\, and foster self-compassion and emotional reconnection. April Pride and invited experts will discuss the limitations of current treatments and how psychedelic therapies could offer new hope. Attendees will leave with a deeper understanding of how psychedelic-assisted therapy might revolutionize the path to healing for those struggling with disordered eating. \nDr. Amanda Downey is a pediatrician and psychiatrist who cares for adolescents and young adults with eating disorders. She works with patients and their families to develop a treatment plan that honors their unique strengths and challenges. Through education\, advocacy\, and research\, she strives to dismantle the systems that increase risk for eating disorders and improve evidence-based eating disorder treatments. She is the assistant medical director of the UCSF Eating Disorders Program and a member of the UCSF Translational Psychedelic Research Program (TrPR).

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: J. Kenji López-Alt and Tessa Lark present Tasting Notes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

If nothing is truly new\, what is creativity? \nIn this special live event\, New York Times bestselling author and James Beard Award–winning chef J. Kenji López-Alt joins Grammy-nominated violinist and composer Tessa Lark for an evening of cooking\, music\, and conversation. Moderated by renowned food writer\, Serious Eats founder\, and Special Sauce host Ed Levine\, the program explores how creatives build on tradition\, transform influence\, and turn identity into expression. \nOn stage\, Kenji brings his signature curiosity to the kitchen\, while Lark\, a Kentucky-born musician who bridges bluegrass roots with classical virtuosity\, reveals how seemingly distant traditions can combine to form brand new sounds. Through live culinary demonstration and musical performance\, this special edition of Kenji’s Tasting Notes reflects on how cultures and ideas blend\, and how originality is less invention and more a reinterpretation of our shared cultural histories.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Lindy West
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

In her new memoir\, Adult Braces\, Lindy West brings readers along on an uproarious cross-country road trip as she unpacks her last few tumultuous years\, rediscovers herself\, and reinvents her marriage. \nThrough Shrill — the book and then the Hulu series — Lindy West became an inspiration. To this day\, she is stopped on the street and hailed as a beacon of empowerment by women who felt badly for not conforming to a narrow set of societal norms—thin\, straight\, compliant. But behind the scenes\, Lindy never felt like she was the self-actualized woman fans made her out to be. When she found herself in the throes of a deep depression\, with her marriage and sense of self-worth hanging in the balance\, she knew she needed to make a change. \nIn Adult Braces\, Lindy shares the story of her rock bottom and of the journey she took to claw her way out of it. With her trademark candor and sense of humor\, she examines her post-Shrill emotional implosion\, her shifting feelings about traditional marriage\, and her search for her long-lost self. She also tracks the highs and lows of her journey\, from eye-opening natural wonders and kitschy roadside attractions to lackluster tourist traps and campground epiphanies. The result is an engaging and laugh-out-loud narrative of becoming as Lindy transforms from a passenger into the active navigator of her own life.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Polymer Clay Earrings Class
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

“Thanks\, I made them myself!” \nThis will be you responding to the many compliments you will get on the fun\, colorful and one of a kind earrings you will make in this workshop! Join WonderSpace Workshops for an afternoon of creativity and community and learn all of the basics of working with polymer clay. This approachable and beginner-friendly medium is very adaptable and lends itself well to so many different styles from funky\, colorful and bold to classic\, sophisticated and polished. You will leave the workshop with several pairs of completed earrings and armed with new skills to continue the crafting journey at home!

item.title: Saturday Family Concert: The Brian Waite Band
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Come sing\, dance\, and laugh with the band that has rocked its way into the hearts of both kids and parents\, as they fly with superheroes and dance on the moon! \nThe Brian Waite Band was born from the idea of creating live rock shows that thrill both parents and children alike. Founded in 2001 by Brian Waite (guitar)\, Todd Gray (drums)\, and Clif Swiggett (bass)\, the trio began touring the West Coast\, and before they knew it\, their music was being played all over the world. Twenty-five years and eight critically-acclaimed albums later\, their interactive shows have evolved into more than just children’s concerts. The Brian Waite Band’s live shows combine musical theater\, imaginative storytelling\, laughs\, and a great rock ‘n roll concert filled with original songs that take the whole family on a “Rock ‘n Roll Adventure.”

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Slavoj Zizek
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join philosopher Slavoj Žižek for a guide to the end times. Is despair the only hope? Can the world escape the clutches of libertarian fascism? Erudite and comic\, ironic and profound\, philosopher Slavoj Žižek has travelled into territory where few of us dare to tread – and at age 76\, he shows no signs of becoming less provocative. As populism continues to grip the globe\, Slavoj joins How To Academy to share his iconoclastic reflections on the state of the world and our prospects for the future. Taking us from Europe and the Middle East into the dark heart of Trump’s America\, he will explore the paradox of ‘liberal fascism’\, peering at the seeping wounds beneath our ‘post-truth age’ to ask: what forms of justice are possible in an age of terrifying disorder? This will be an evening of dark humour to help us survive in a dark era\, with Trump\, Putin\, postmodernism\, and antisemitism all on the cards. Slavoj promises to tell us many things we do not want to hear — and to remind us why he is widely known as the ‘most dangerous philosopher of the West’. Don’t miss it. Slavoj Žižek will sign copies of Liberal Fascisms following the event. Events are offered for informational\, entertainment\, and educational purposes only. Read Town Hall’s Program Content Policy.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Painting Class: Watercolor Landscapes for Beginners
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

This step-by-step\, instructor-led watercolor class is designed for beginners who want to learn how to paint beautiful landscapes with confidence. No prior experience is needed. You’ll be guided through the essential techniques used in watercolor landscape painting\, including: Basic and graded washes Composition fundamentals Layering to create depth and atmosphere Color mixing for natural\, harmonious tones With hands-on practice and individual guidance\, you will learn how to build a painting from start to finish and create your own completed landscape by the end of the session. The class is held in a relaxed and supportive environment\, making it a comfortable and encouraging space for total beginners. All materials and supplies are provided\, including a take-home watercolor kit (valued at $20) so you can continue practicing beyond the classroom. This class will take place at Swoon City.

From My Ballard

item.title: V.E. Schwab and Cat Clarke with Moira Macdonald
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Layering classic tropes of the mystery genre with a clever modern satire of the publishing industry\, The Ending Writes Itself is the debut novel of “Evelyn Clarke\,” a new writing collaboration between best-selling authors V.E. Schwab and Cat Clarke. Joined in conversation by local author and journalist Moira Macdonald\, Schwab and Clarke explore their co-authoring venture and the twists and turns that await readers in their new take on a locked-room mystery thriller. How will the pages unfold for the six authors isolated with nothing but their own minds\, a diabolical deadline\, and a plot-driving incentive? Starting is often the hardest part. But getting to the end could be murder. \nWorld-renowned best-selling novelist Arthur Fletch is a reclusive genius who captivates readers with iconic protagonists and fiendish twists. So when six struggling authors of different genres receive an invitation to spend a weekend on his private island off the coast of Scotland\, they pack their bags. But the twists have gone further than ever this time\, and the authors are greeted with a shocking secret– Arthur Fletch is dead… and his highly-anticipated last novel remains unfinished. \nDesperate to publish\, Fletch’s agent and editor have summoned these writers hoping one of them can pen a worthy ending. To sweeten the deal\, they pitch them the opportunity of a lifetime. Ghost write the final chapter and win an irresistible prize– a mind-boggling sum of money and a partnership that could relaunch their own careers and guarantee future bestsellers. And with that to motivate them\, surely 72 hours is enough time to finish Fletch’s magnus opus? All they have to do is write…

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: Design for Longevity: ADUs and Aging in Place
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Mehdi Reading Room\, 1119 8th Ave\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join Lili Weigert\, author of Making Space: ADUs for Modern Living\, in conversation with local ADU designers Joe Schneider and Mike Freeman of JAS Design Build. They will explore how thoughtful ADU design can support aging in place\, foster intergenerational living\, and provide resilient housing options for the future. Rebecca Crichton\, Executive Director of Northwest Center for Creative Aging\, will moderate the discussion.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Romance Writing Class
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

CLASSES WILL TAKE PLACE ON 4/7\, 4/14\, 4/21\, & 4/28 \nAre you a romance reader eager to learn how to write the genre you love? In this four-week class (eight hours of work total)\, you’ll learn the basics of plotting and writing a romance novel from scratch. No matter what romance subgenre calls your name\, you’ll learn to expand an initial idea\, center a romantic arc\, and build strong\, complementary characters. You’ll leave this collaborative\, workshop-based class with a plan for a novel (including character exercises\, a beat sheet\, and a synopsis) and at least one chapter of written prose. You’ll also get an overview of resources available to you once you finish your novel.

From My Ballard

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Macrame Class: Keychain Wristlet
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

In this workshop with Angie Cooper of My Moon Macrame you will be taught the basic knotting techniques used in macrame. This class is great for beginners and intermediate levels. This project is designed to be altered to cater to students at any skill level. We provide craft supplies\, video tutorials and one-on-one guidance to help you create your macrame project. During this class you will learn to macrame a lanyard keychain. Your instructor\, Angie from My Moon Macrame will teach you the knots and provide step by step instructions to help you complete your project. We provide all craft supplies\, video tutorials and one-on-one guidance. This project is designed to cater to students at any skill level. You will go home with a completed macrame lanyard keychain and all the tools you need to continue your macrame journey at home. Supplies: 4mm Oeko Tex Recycled Cotton Cord Key Ring Scissors Tape

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Magnolia Flea Market
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Magnolia Flea Market\, 4257 24th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Discover a curated mix of vintage finds\, local makers\, handmade goods\, sweet treats\, coffee\, and food—plus a vintage warehouse full of gems to explore. \nWhether you’re hunting for one-of-a-kind pieces or just enjoying the Magnolia neighborhood\, this is your weekend spot. Bring a friend\, bring your pup\, and come hang out.

From Discover Magnolia

Event details

item.title: Glass Class: Book Cover Coasters
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Cozy-up with a mug of tea and your favorite book… but oh no! Where are all the coasters? Have no fear! We’ve got just the solution to protect your precious objects from destructive heat and moisture — glass book coasters. That’s right! Coasters designed by you to look just like your favorite book cover or classic novels\, so that you can enjoy your next book with some extra special\, on-theme decor. \nIn this 3-hour glass charm workshop\, you will build unique compositions using Elmer’s glue and a variety of glass dots\, stringers\, shapes\, and more. Learn the fundamentals of fusing while experimenting with a new medium and exploring the many possibilities! Cost includes all tools and supplies as well as materials for up to 2 coasters per person. Finished pieces will be ready for pick-up at the workshop location after 1 week.

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: History Café: Preserving the Vision of Asahel Curtis
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join Margaret Weatherbee\, Head of Collections at the Washington State Historical Society and History Museum\, for an in-depth look at the digitization and preservation of the Asahel Curtis Collection. This collection features over 42\,000 images by photographer Asahel Curtis (1873-1941) that document the evolution of life in the Pacific Northwest in the early 20th century. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 11 AM

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Art Class: Handmade Water Marbled Paper
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Step into the mesmerizing world of water marbling\, a centuries-old art technique where color floats\, swirls\, and dances across the surface of water before being transformed into stunning\, one-of-a-kind designs. In this immersive\, hands-on workshop\, you’ll learn how to create beautiful marbled patterns by manipulating paint on water and transferring your designs onto paper and other surfaces. You’ll experiment with color\, movement\, and tools to create unique marbled prints on paper\, fabric\, and small 3-D objects. This class is designed for beginners and curious creatives alike – no prior art experience is needed. All supplies are provided\, and step-by-step guidance will ensure everyone can successfully create multiple finished pieces to take home. Along the way\, we’ll talk about troubleshooting common challenges and how to continue marbling at home with accessible materials. Water marbling is both meditative and playful\, inviting experimentation and embracing the unexpected. Come ready to slow down\, get a little messy\, and enjoy the process of creating something truly unique. The class will cover the following: 1. The four foundational patterns (stone\, chevron\, getgel\, and nonpareil) 2. Tools\, materials\, and how to prepare for a water marbling session 3. Historical grounding into this art form 4. Water marbling examples from around the world 5. Tips and tricks for water marbling at home

From My Ballard

Event details

item.title: Meet the Cossacks: Seattle's Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Dive into the history of the Cossacks\, Seattle’s long strolling Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team\, with Rob Root and other members. Since 1938\, the Seattle Cossacks have captured the imagination of audiences with great pyramids and other wonders of the motorcycling world. Following the presentation\, visitors are welcome to get up close and personal for photos with the team’s vintage Harleys! \nTickets for this program are free\, but do not include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. If you would like to see all that MOHAI has to offer during your visit\, you can purchase museum admission here. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels\, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th (37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend\, starting at 9:00 AM) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle\, WA 98199) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group\, please email communications@unitedindians.org

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Swoon City\, 1716 NW Market St\, Seattle\, WA\, 98107\, United States

Let your creativity flow with instruction from local artist Brooke Westlund to create your own 16×20″ art piece. This is a fun class whether or not you haven’t painted since you were a kid or if you are an experienced artist. Brooke will lead you through the steps to create a one of a kind painting and teach you tools and tricks along the way\, you can pick your own colors and your piece will be unique to you! Class is about 2.5 hours.

item.title: Paving the Way: Bicycle Infrastructure Tour
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Offsite

Join Merlin Rainwater\, creator of Seattle’s Infamous Redline Guide\, and Tom Fucoloro\, Founder and Editor of the Seattle Bike Blog and author of Biking Uphill in the Rain: The Story of Seattle from behind the Handlebars\, for a bicycle tour exploring the history of Seattle bicycle infrastructure. \nThis tour requires riding a bicycle between multiple sites in Seattle. Please bring your own bike. Helmets and lights will be required. For any accessibility support\, or general inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Interbay Summer Camps- The Little Gym
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Little Gym- Interbay\, 2213 15th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Interbay summer camps are 3 hours of Serious Fun for kiddos 3-10 years of age! Camp consists of 2 hours of gym time\, and 1 hour of snacks and craft time. Every week brings a new and exciting theme for your camper and we always have blocks\, books and coloring pages on standby for those campers that need a break. Camps are held Monday-Wednesday from 1-4pm. We offer special discounted pricing for members and camp pack purchases. Non member pricing is $75 for a single camp pass. More information on pack pricing can be found via our scannable QR code. Priority registration opens February 1st.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. The plants grown here nourishes our elders\, youth\, families\, and ceremonies. Help us build a future rooted in culture\, care\, and connection! 🍓 \nQuestions? Email Shameka.Gagnier@gmail.com or call/text (720) 212 7113 📞

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: History Café: Field Notes Podcast
item.description:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join HistoryLink’s Tony Williams for a live recording of the Field Notes podcast featuring UW Architecture Professor Vikram Prakash. We’re digging into the intersection of deep time and urban design to discover how Seattle’s geological past continues to shape its future. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Drawing From History: Ripples of Time
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Lake Union is haunted by ghosts of the past\, echoing through time. In this class\, participants will explore past\, present\, and future of the Lake Union Watershed by creating quick layered gesture drawings in charcoal\, based off photos and ephemera from MOHAI’s collections. The class will culminate at the water’s edge\, creating a drawing representing visions for the future. \nDrawing From History is offered in partnership with Gage Academy of Art. \nAll art making materials will be provided. Tickets include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. For accessibility support and general program inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry
